title: ModelConfig + component registry types
description:
Create the synapse-inference crate with ModelConfig deserialization and component registry trait definitions.

New crate: crates/synapse-inference/
- Cargo.toml with dependencies: serde, serde_json, synapse-core, synapse-sys

Config types (src/config/):
- model_config.rs: ModelConfig struct deserializing from JSON. Nested configs for architecture, attention, norm, FFN, position, quantization.
- attention.rs: AttentionConfig enum (GQA, MHA, MQA, SlidingWindow) with params
- norm.rs: NormConfig enum (RMSNorm, LayerNorm)
- ffn.rs: FFNConfig enum (SwiGLU, GELU, GeGLU)
- position.rs: PositionConfig enum (RoPE, Learned, Sinusoidal)
- quantization.rs: QuantConfig enum (F32, F16, INT8 with calibration)
- mod.rs: re-exports

Registry traits (src/registry/):
- AttentionVariant, NormVariant, FFNVariant, PositionVariant trait definitions
- factory.rs: Config → Box<dyn Trait> factory functions
- mod.rs: re-exports

src/lib.rs: Public API, prelude module

Model config JSON files:
- configs/qwen3_0.6b.json
- configs/llama3.2_1b.json
- configs/mistral_7b.json

Tests (mandatory):
- Deserialize Qwen3-0.6B config from JSON, verify all fields
- Deserialize LLaMA config, verify different values
- Factory creates correct variant for each config enum
- Unknown config values produce clear errors
- Round-trip: serialize → deserialize → compare

Pass/fail:
- Qwen3 config parses correctly
- Factory returns correct trait objects
- Serde errors are descriptive
